habitat: Subtropical/tropical moist montane forests; damp well-shaded montane forest
story: Discover the stunning Whihead Trogon (*Harpactes whiteheadi*), a member of the diverse Trogonidae family. Native to Subtropical/tropical moist montane forests; damp well-shaded montane forest, this bird faces various environmental challenges. Currently classified as Near Threatened (NT) on the IUCN Red List, the Whihead Trogon represents the delicate balance of its native region. Understanding its habitat requirements and distribution is key to effective conservation planning. This species contributes to the richness of avian diversity, and this poster offers an artistic glimpse into its world.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
